title: TC003-test get project details api
description: Verify that the GET /api/projects/[id] endpoint returns the correct project details including title, description, members, tags, and progress metrics for an authenticated user.
code:
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:3000"
LOGIN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/api/auth"
PROJECTS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects"

TEST_USER_EMAIL = "testuser_tc003@example.com"
TEST_USER_PASSWORD = "TestPass123!"
TEST_USER_NAME = "Test User TC003"

def test_get_project_details_api():
    session = requests.Session()
    session.timeout = 30

    # Register user (ignore error if already registered)
    try:
        register_payload = {
            "action": "register",
            "name": TEST_USER_NAME,
            "email": TEST_USER_EMAIL,
            "password": TEST_USER_PASSWORD
        }
        resp = session.post(LOGIN_URL, json=register_payload, timeout=30)
        if resp.status_code not in (200, 201, 409):
            resp.raise_for_status()
    except requests.RequestException as e:
        # Proceed if user already exists (409 Conflict)
        if resp.status_code != 409:
            raise e

    # Login user to get session cookie
    login_payload = {
        "action": "login",
        "email": TEST_USER_EMAIL,
        "password": TEST_USER_PASSWORD
    }
    login_resp = session.post(LOGIN_URL, json=login_payload, timeout=30)
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, f"Login failed with status {login_resp.status_code}"
    assert 'session_token' in session.cookies, "No session_token cookie received"

    # Create a new project to get a valid project id
    create_project_payload = {
        "title": "Test Project TC003",
        "description": "Project created for TC003 testing",
        "color_label": "blue",
        "members": [TEST_USER_EMAIL],
        "tags": ["test", "tc003"],
        "progress": {
            "tasks_total": 10,
            "tasks_completed": 4,
            "percent_complete": 40
        }
    }
    create_resp = session.post(PROJECTS_URL, json=create_project_payload, timeout=30)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201, f"Project creation failed with status {create_resp.status_code}"
    project = create_resp.json()
    project_id = project.get("id")
    assert project_id, "Created project response missing 'id'"

    try:
        # Fetch the project details via GET /api/projects/[id]
        get_resp = session.get(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=30)
        assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"Get project failed with status {get_resp.status_code}"
        data = get_resp.json()

        # Assert required fields
        assert "title" in data and data["title"] == create_project_payload["title"], "Title mismatch"
        assert "description" in data and data["description"] == create_project_payload["description"], "Description mismatch"
        assert "members" in data and isinstance(data["members"], list), "Missing or invalid members"
        assert TEST_USER_EMAIL in data["members"], "Test user not in members"
        assert "tags" in data and set(data["tags"]) == set(create_project_payload["tags"]), "Tags mismatch"
        assert "progress" in data and isinstance(data["progress"], dict), "Missing or invalid progress"
        progress = data["progress"]
        assert progress.get("tasks_total") == create_project_payload["progress"]["tasks_total"], "tasks_total mismatch"
        assert progress.get("tasks_completed") == create_project_payload["progress"]["tasks_completed"], "tasks_completed mismatch"
        assert progress.get("percent_complete") == create_project_payload["progress"]["percent_complete"], "percent_complete mismatch"

    finally:
        # Cleanup: delete the created project
        del_resp = session.delete(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=30)
        assert del_resp.status_code in (200, 204), f"Failed to delete project with status {del_resp.status_code}"


test_get_project_details_api()

title: TC004-test update project api with valid data
description: Verify that the PUT /api/projects/[id] endpoint successfully updates the project title, description, and tags when valid data is provided by an authenticated user.
code:
import requests
import uuid

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:3000"
TIMEOUT = 30

def test_update_project_api_with_valid_data():
    session = requests.Session()

    # Credentials for authentication - replace with valid test credentials
    email = "testuser@example.com"
    password = "TestPass123!"

    # Authenticate user to obtain session cookie
    auth_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/auth/login"
    auth_payload = {"email": email, "password": password}
    auth_headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}

    auth_response = session.post(auth_url, json=auth_payload, headers=auth_headers, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    assert auth_response.status_code == 200, f"Authentication failed with status code {auth_response.status_code}"
    assert 'session_token' in session.cookies, "session_token cookie not found after login"

    # Helper function to create a new project
    def create_project():
        url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects"
        project_data = {
            "title": f"Initial Project {uuid.uuid4()}",
            "description": "Initial project description",
            "color": "blue",
            "tags": ["initial", "test"],
            "members": [],
            "progress": 0
        }
        resp = session.post(url, json=project_data, timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Project creation failed with status code {resp.status_code}"
        project = resp.json()
        assert "id" in project, "No project id returned on creation"
        return project["id"]

    # Helper function to delete a project by id
    def delete_project(project_id):
        url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects/{project_id}"
        resp = session.delete(url, timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert resp.status_code == 204 or resp.status_code == 200, f"Project deletion failed with status code {resp.status_code}"

    project_id = create_project()

    try:
        # Prepare update data
        update_url = f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects/{project_id}"
        update_data = {
            "title": "Updated Project Title",
            "description": "Updated project description",
            "tags": ["updated", "api", "test"]
        }
        update_headers = {"Content-Type": "application/json"}

        # Update the project
        update_response = session.put(update_url, json=update_data, headers=update_headers, timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert update_response.status_code == 200, f"Update failed with status code {update_response.status_code}"
        updated_project = update_response.json()
        assert updated_project["title"] == update_data["title"], "Project title not updated correctly"
        assert updated_project["description"] == update_data["description"], "Project description not updated correctly"
        assert set(updated_project["tags"]) == set(update_data["tags"]), "Project tags not updated correctly"

        # Verify update by fetching project details
        get_response = session.get(update_url, timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert get_response.status_code == 200, f"Fetching updated project failed with status code {get_response.status_code}"
        project_details = get_response.json()
        assert project_details["title"] == update_data["title"], "Fetched project title mismatch after update"
        assert project_details["description"] == update_data["description"], "Fetched project description mismatch after update"
        assert set(project_details["tags"]) == set(update_data["tags"]), "Fetched project tags mismatch after update"

    finally:
        # Clean up: delete the created project
        delete_project(project_id)

test_update_project_api_with_valid_data()

title: TC005-test delete project api success
description: Verify that the DELETE /api/projects/[id] endpoint successfully deletes the project from the database.
code:
import requests
import uuid

BASE_URL = "http://localhost:3000"
LOGIN_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/api/auth/login"
PROJECTS_URL = f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects"
TIMEOUT = 30

# Use test credentials - ensure these exist or adjust accordingly
TEST_EMAIL = "testuser@example.com"
TEST_PASSWORD = "Password123!"


def login_and_get_session():
    """Login to get a session cookie."""
    login_payload = {
        "email": TEST_EMAIL,
        "password": TEST_PASSWORD
    }
    session = requests.Session()
    response = session.post(LOGIN_URL, json=login_payload, headers={'Content-Type': 'application/json'}, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    assert response.status_code == 200, f"Login failed: {response.text}"
    # Session cookies are handled automatically by requests.Session
    return session


def create_project(session):
    """Create a new project for test and return its ID."""
    unique_title = f"Test Project {uuid.uuid4()}"
    project_payload = {
        "title": unique_title,
        "description": "Temporary project for deletion test",
        "color": "blue",
        "members": [],
        "tags": [],
        "progress": 0
    }
    response = session.post(f"{BASE_URL}/api/projects", json=project_payload, timeout=TIMEOUT)
    assert response.status_code == 201, f"Create project failed: {response.text}"
    project = response.json()
    project_id = project.get("id")
    assert project_id, "Created project response missing 'id'"
    return project_id


def test_delete_project_api_success():
    session = login_and_get_session()
    project_id = None
    try:
        # Create a new project for deletion test
        project_id = create_project(session)

        # Verify project exists by fetching details
        get_resp = session.get(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert get_resp.status_code == 200, f"Get project failed: {get_resp.text}"

        # Delete the project
        delete_resp = session.delete(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert delete_resp.status_code == 200 or delete_resp.status_code == 204, f"Delete project failed: {delete_resp.text}"

        # Verify deletion by attempting to get the project again
        get_after_delete_resp = session.get(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=TIMEOUT)
        assert get_after_delete_resp.status_code == 404, f"Project not deleted, still accessible: {get_after_delete_resp.text}"

        # Mark project_id None since already deleted
        project_id = None
    finally:
        # Cleanup in case deletion failed
        if project_id:
            # Attempt delete cleanup quietly
            session.delete(f"{PROJECTS_URL}/{project_id}", timeout=TIMEOUT)


test_delete_project_api_success()
